turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough for two weeks now, and it's really starting to get in the way of my daily life - I'm an elementary school teacher, so I need to be able to take care of myself. The cough is productive, with yellowish-green sputum.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: It's this persistent and annoying cough that just won't go away.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about two weeks ago and has been getting worse over time.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all day, every day. I've had to take a few days off work because of it.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's around an 8 or 9 out of 10 - I'm really struggling to manage my daily activities and get a good night's sleep due to this constant coughing.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yeah, besides the cough, I also have difficulty breathing and wheezing, especially after exertion. It feels like my airways are constricted, making it hard for me to catch my breath at times.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yeah, it definitely gets worse with deep breathing and coughing - that's when I feel a sharp pain on my right side.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I do experience low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), which is usually worse in the evenings.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it's just localized to my right side when I cough or breathe deeply.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any noticeable swelling in my legs or feet.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage my symptoms with over-the-counter dextromethorphan for a cough suppressant, but it hasn't provided much relief. I'm also taking cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any known medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I actually did have a routine physical exam about a year ago and everything was normal.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I'm a social drinker, so I do occasionally have 1-2 glasses of wine on weekends. And no, I've never smoked.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: I've been feeling a bit off lately and my appetite has been decreased over the past few days. It's making it hard for me to keep up with my usual daily routine.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mom actually has asthma.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer, but that's about it.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been about a year since my last physical exam. I've been putting off seeing a doctor for this cough because it's getting worse and interfering with my life, so I'm here now!

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I do have an allergy to penicillin - it causes me to break out in a rash.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: You know, I've been feeling really fatigued and just overall unwell for weeks now. It's not just the cough that's bothering me, it's like my whole body feels drained of energy. And to be honest, I'm getting a bit worried about how long this will last and if I'll ever get better.
